title: Euchiton breviscapus
intro: Euchiton breviscapus, commonly known as the New Zealand broom or short-stalked Euchiton, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and it is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular protective effects. The bioactive constituents of Euchiton breviscapus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. Euchiton breviscapus is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and high blood pressure.
